en: Hyperbaric Work

Qualified supervision is required, and pressurized air work must be conducted under the supervision of a reliable, technically competent, and medically suitable supervisor or deputy.\nCompressed Air and Diving Work Ordinance, Section 5
Qualified Supervision
Advance notification is required, and labor inspectorate notification must occur at least 14 days before work begins.\nCompressed Air and Diving Work Ordinance, Section 7(1), 47(1)1
Notification to Labor Inspectorate (Pre-Work)

Employees must be at least 21 and medically fit for pressurized air work. Persons over 40 without prior compressed air experience must not be newly assigned and persons over 50 must not perform hyperbaric work. Fitness certificate issuance is required and physician confirmation must be received before assignment.\nCompressed Air and Diving Work Ordinance, Section 8(1), 8(4)
Medical Fitness Examinations (Pre-Work, After Work)
Pre-work hazard instruction is required and employees must receive hyperbaric hazard instruction before starting work.\nCompressed Air and Diving Work Ordinance, Section 8(3), 24(2), 47(1)1
Training (Pre-Work and Every 6 Months)
A self-contained breathing apparatus is required, and one unit per worker must be provided above 1.1 kp/cm².\nCompressed Air and Diving Work Ordinance, Section 11(6)
Self-Contained Breathing Apparatus (SCBA) Above Pressure Threshold (>1.1 kp/cm²)
Recompression chamber provision is required above 1.1 kp/cm².\nCompressed Air and Diving Work Ordinance, Section 14(1)
Recompression Chamber (>1.1 kp/cm²)
Maximum exposure duration limitations are required and compressed air time must follow pressure-based hourly limits.\nCompressed Air and Diving Work Ordinance, Section 26(1)
Maximum Daily Exposure Durations

A designated lock keeper is required, and each personnel or combined lock must be operated by a lock keeper. Lock keepers may only admit persons employed in pressurized air work if they can demonstrate their suitability for such work.\nCompressed Air and Diving Work Ordinance, Section 24(1), 24(3)
Access Control

Applicable vessels and pressure systems must undergo initial inspection and hydrostatic (water-pressure) testing before first use. Internal inspection and hydrotesting must be performed after major repairs or modifications, re-inspection is required when prior tests are over three years old or when relocated to a new site, and pressure leak testing must be conducted on full systems before work begins.\nCompressed Air and Diving Work Ordinance, Section 21
Qualified Inspections and Testing (Initial, Pre-Work, After Repairs, Every 3 Years)

Inspection records are required and results must be recorded in designated registers. Regulatory records must be maintained and accessible to inspectorate and accident authorities.\nCompressed Air and Diving Work Ordinance, Section 21(6), 47(1)5, 47(2), 50(1)
Records
First aid availability must be ensured, and trained persons must be accessible during operations.\nCompressed Air and Diving Work Ordinance, Section 24(5-6)
Trained First Aid Personnel Available
